title: rhinanthus divaricatus
intro: Rhinanthus divaricatus, also known as the dog's thumb, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, and is used to treat conditions such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving the immune system. Rhinanthus divaricatus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
